transcript: This is a 7-minute daily yoga routine for beginners that you can easily do at home. It is compact yet extremely rejuvenating for the body and mind. If you have never done any of the yogic breathing exercises or asanas, then this can be a perfect start. One more thing, this video is not just to watch but to follow along. So come on, let's do it together. Hello friends, welcome to FitTuber. Every morning, after waking up, have a glass of water, freshen up and get ready to do yoga, preferably outdoors, without eating anything. All you would need is a yoga mat and an open mind to start with. If you do not have a yoga mat, you can even use a bed sheet or a towel. Facing East, sit down in Sukhasana, Arthasiddhasana, Siddhasana or Padmasana, whatever you are comfortable in. For the next 7 minutes, let's immerse ourselves completely in this divine practice. We will start with chanting OM three times. Spine erect, neck straight, hands in dhyana mudra, close your eyes. Take a deep breath in and exhale out, chanting OM. OM is not a sound of any one particular religion. It is the sound of creation, which encompasses all sounds within. Om is the sound of oneness. The vibrations of this sound harmonizes the body for a deep yoga session. Relax, keep your eyes closed. Now we will do Bhastrika, also known as Bellows Breath for 30 seconds. Alright, deeply breathe in, forcefully breathe out, in, out, in, out, maintain the same rhythm, breathing in and out through your nostrils. This is the yogic breath of fire, which energizes the body almost instantly. Do it with undivided attention, feel that with every inhalation, positive energy is entering your body, and with every exhalation, the negativity is being pushed out. Relax. Keep your eyes closed. Now for the next two minutes, we will do Kapalbhati, also called rapid exhalation. I will start with counting and with every count, you have to exhale out through your nostrils. Take a deep breath in and 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7. Maintain the same rhythm. You can keep counting in your mind if you so wish. However, at no point overexert yourself. Keep a smile on your face radiating positivity. Throughout this kriya, keep your attention fixed on the abdominal area. It will enhance its effect many folds. This is where the Manipurka Chakra is located. Feel that your abdomen is being massaged. The subtle channels are being opened up. The toxins are being pushed out. The internal factory is being cleansed. This is the Yogic lung purification. Just keep breathing out. It so effectively circulates the blood in the entire body that in yogic scriptures, kapalbhati has been equated to sprinting. Do it with complete involvement. Focus fixed on the abdominal area. Over 95% of the diseases originate from the stomach, which is why regular practice of Kapalbhati has such a profound effect on health the 72 000 nadis or the subtle channels of which we are made of kapalbhati has the power to clear its pathways since kapalbhati helps detoxifies the complete mind-body system when consistently practiced,, the face shines which is also why it is named so. Kapala meaning the skull and Bhati meaning shining. Simple yet an advanced practice for overall well-being. Relax. Now for the next 2 minutes we will do alternate nostril breathing commonly commonly known as Anulom Vilom Pranayam. Such a calming exercise. Using the right thumb, block the right nostril. Inhale through the left nostril for 4 seconds. Hold there for 4 seconds. Then placing the middle 2 fingers, close the left nostril and exhale through the right nostril for four seconds. In the same position inhale through the right nostril for four seconds. Hold for four seconds. Place the right thumb to block the right nostril and exhale Inhale through the left nostril for 4 seconds, repeat 3 4 hold 2 3 4 exhale 2 3 4 inhale 2 3 2-3-4 Exhale 2-3-4 Maintain the same rhythm, keeping a mild focus in between your eyebrows. This is an advanced form of alternate nostril breathing. Long inhalation oxygenates the blood, breath retention strengthens the nervous system and I'll see you next time. form of alternate nostril breathing. Long inhalation oxygenates the blood, breath retention strengthens the nervous system and long exhalations eliminate carbon dioxide. Also called the Nadi Shodhan Pranayam as it purifies the two most important nadis, Ida and Pingala. stress, anxiety, depression, you name it. Anulom Vilom has the capacity to destroy it from its root. Do it with total involvement. Feel the system getting revitalized. Feel the impurities are being brought out. Your mind is getting lighter. such deep cleansing and long belong the ultimate therapy. The last breathing exercise of this session is the Brahmari or the B-Breath. Thumbs closing the ears, index fingers just above your eyebrows and the rest of your fingers softly placed over your eyes. Touch the tip of your tongue to the palate, mouth closed. Focus in between your eyebrows. Inhale deeply through your nostril for 4 seconds and exhale making a humming sound of OM for 8 seconds. Inhale 2, 3, 4. The vibrations of this divine sound balances the body and mind, cleansing the aura. that your body is radiating positive rays in the form of humming sound of the bee activating the pineal and pituitary glands it is an elixir for all brain disorders now before opening the eyes bring your palms together in front of your face and rub them with vigour for about 10 seconds. This will stimulate the acupressure points allowing better flow of prana. Place the palms softly over the eyes, feel the warmth, it heals the eyes. Now slowly open your eyes. This is the moment of tranquility, calm, harmony. Thank you. and as you exhale, bend your knees, interlock your fingers, lift your head to first touch your nose and then chin. Hold there for 10 seconds. Enhancing the blood circulation in the pelvic region, this relieves excess wind in the system. Relax. Now we will do Markatasana. Bend your knees and keep your feet close to hips, spread your arms wide, now turn your knees together to right, while neck to left, hold there for 10 seconds, breathe normally, feel your back, neck and shoulder muscles getting stretched, now move your knees to the left, while the neck to right again hold for 10 seconds market asana is probably the best remedial posture for back pain relax keep lying down on the mat and shavasana for about 30 seconds it It is the yogic cool down. Close your eyes and feel the sensations in the body. Be aware of every moment. Your mind and body is thanking you for having completed this yoga session. Every organ, every cell in your body is feeling delighted to have been taken care of. Be proud of yourself. You have done it. I bow down to the divine within you. I'll see you tomorrow. Namaste. So friends, how was it? I hope you enjoyed this yoga session. This might be a 7 minute yoga routine but do not underestimate its effectiveness. Especially if you do three things. One, try to keep your eyes closed throughout the session. Second, be comfortable and try to avoid unnecessary movement. Third and the most important, try to do it with undivided attention and focus. Then I promise you will feel a difference in one week. However, the key lies in consistent practice. You will thank yourself for fitting it into your tight schedule. If you found this video helpful and if you would like to see a 7-minute yogic workout, then please do let me know by giving this video a big thumbs up. Check out Ayuga's 100% Kumkumadi Skin Radiance Face Oil. Turn the pack and you will find that it is an authentic formulation made using saffron, sandalwood, goat milk, lotus, and 20 other precious herbs that lift the face glow naturally. The reference Ayurvedic text is also clearly mentioned. To use it, take an adequate amount, rub gently, and apply evenly onto your face. Company recommends that you apply it in a special face posture. Known for its anti-aging properties, this natural moisturizer is suitable for all skin types. To buy Ayuga's USTA Organic Ayush Licensed Made Safe and Cruelty-Free 100% Kumkumadi Skin Radiance Face Oil, click on the link down in the description box. Do not forget to apply the special discount coupon to get 20% off on your order. So friends that's all for this video, please do remember to subscribe to my channel and hit that bell icon so that you never miss a video from me. You can also follow me on Instagram. My name is Vivek, I thank you so much for watching.
